prompt:
A generic medical-product instruction illustration in simple black line-art on a pure white background, in the universal patient-information-leaflet style. Subject: two hands meeting at the center of the frame to INSERT a small UNCAPPED glass vial UPWARD into the matching slot on the body of a round white blood-collection device — captured MID-ACTION, with a clear arrow indicating the direction of insertion. The vial end and the device's slot end are COMPLEMENTARY mirror shapes that interlock when joined.

VIAL — replicate the vial from the provided reference image PIXEL-FAITHFULLY (same scale within the lower hand, same line weight, same hand pose, same body proportions, same asymmetric stepped open rim, same double-line graduation ring, same empty transparent interior, same finger-sized slim glass tube). Critical features that MUST appear exactly as in the reference:
- VIAL OPEN STEPPED RIM at top: STRONGLY asymmetric. One half (back) raised as a SHORT BLUNT ANGULAR tongue, flush with the side wall (no overhang, no arch), height ≈ half its width. Other half (front) flat and lower. Clean vertical step in the middle of the rim. Peak of the raised half is BLUNT — not a smooth curve, not domed — a defined flat-ish top with crisp shoulders.
- DOUBLE-LINE graduation ring: two thin parallel horizontal strokes ≈ one line-weight apart wrap fully around the body at roughly 55-60% up from the base, drawn as a flattened ellipse.
- EMPTY interior: no meniscus, no hatching, no liquid, nothing inside the glass.
- Tiny slim tube, height ≈ 2x diameter, finger-width.
- Faint single-line glass highlight stripe on one side.

LOWER HAND: enters from the lower-right ascending — same hand pose as the reference image. Thumb on the near side, index/middle fingers wrapped around the far side, holding the tiny vial vertically with the open stepped rim pointing UP. Wrist trails off at the lower-right frame edge.

UPPER HAND: enters from the upper-left descending. Grips a round white plastic blood-collection device — thumb on the near side, fingers wrapped around the far side. The device is oriented so its TOP face (with a LARGE PROMINENT SOLID RED CIRCLE blood-collection hub — see device geometry below) tilts UP toward the viewer in 3/4 view. The device's CONNECTOR END (where the vial inserts) faces DOWN toward the rising vial. Only the lower portion of the upper hand is visible; wrist trails off at the upper-left frame edge.

DEVICE GEOMETRY:
- A short squat round white plastic cylinder (like a small puck), drawn in 3/4 view.
- TOP FACE: a LARGE PROMINENT SOLID RED CIRCLE fills most of the top — this is the blood-collection hub, the puncture target. Draw it as a flat round disc filled SOLID RED (red is the only color in the image — every other element remains pure black line-art on white). The red circle's diameter is roughly 70-80% of the device's overall top-face diameter, centered. The thin white-plastic rim around the red circle is the only non-red portion of the top. The red hub has no other markings on it.
- CONNECTOR END (facing DOWN toward the vial): this is the END of the cylinder pointing down. It is NOT a rectangular slot. It is a CIRCULAR connector port — a round opening sized to perfectly match the outer diameter of the vial's open stepped rim, so the vial slides up into it with a precise fit. Draw the connector end as: a circular face (in 3/4 perspective so it reads as a flattened ellipse), with a CIRCULAR / ROUND OPENING in its center matching the vial's diameter (drawn as a smaller flattened ellipse inside the larger one, suggesting depth — the inner ellipse is the rim of the opening, with darkness/shadow inside indicating the hollow port). A thin chrome / metal collar ellipse is drawn just inside the opening as the connector fitting.
- STEPPED TONGUE ON THE CONNECTOR END (matches the vial's stepped rim, mirror-complementary): the CONNECTOR END's circular face is itself ASYMMETRIC — one half of its circular edge extends DOWNWARD as a SHORT BLUNT ANGULAR tongue (flush with the device's side wall, height ≈ half its width, peak blunt and angular, not smooth/domed), and the OTHER half of the edge is a flat shorter lip. CRITICAL: the raised tongue on the DEVICE is on the OPPOSITE side from the VIAL's raised tongue, so when the vial slides up into the circular opening, the vial's raised half tucks into the device's recessed flat half, and the device's raised half tucks into the vial's recessed flat half — they interlock like two complementary jaws meeting. Both raised tongues are pointed at each other but on opposite sides of the central insertion axis.
- No other markings, no logo, no number, no label, no extra holes/buttons/spouts on the device's side or bottom edges.

ALIGNMENT: the vial's stepped rim and the device's stepped connector end are vertically aligned on the same axis, with a small visible gap of a few millimeters between them showing the mid-action moment. The viewer must IMMEDIATELY see that the two stepped ends are MIRROR-COMPLEMENTARY and will fit together perfectly when joined — raised tongue on one side of the vial, raised tongue on the OPPOSITE side of the device. Make this mirror relationship visually obvious — if you imagine sliding the device straight down onto the vial, the raised halves would slot into the opposing recessed halves with no collision.

ARROW: include a SINGLE clean black arrow in the empty white space BETWEEN the vial's rim and the device's slot — a slim straight vertical arrow with a clear arrowhead pointing UP, positioned just to the side of the gap (so it does not overlap the vial, the device, or the hand). The arrow clearly indicates that the vial is being pushed UP into the device. Use the same line weight as the rest of the illustration.

ABSOLUTE SCALE: hands DOMINATE the canvas. The round device is palm-sized (fits in the upper hand). The vial is finger-sized — small, slim. Hands are visibly the largest elements.

Style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on clean white. The ONLY color anywhere in the image is the SOLID RED FILL of the round blood-collection hub on top of the device — every other element (hands, vial, device body, arrow, lines, hatching) is pure black on white. Minimal short parallel hatching only for soft shadow / volume on the hand knuckles. No frame, no border, no caption, no labels, no logos, no numbers, no other decorative elements. The arrow is the ONLY exception to 'no arrows' in this image — it is a required instructional element here. Subject centered in a 1:1 square composition with generous clean white margins. Drawn-by-hand line work, generic patient information leaflet illustration style.
